name: Gannon Storm
notes: First G5 in 20 years. Aurora visible as far south as Florida and Mexico. Named after physicist Jennifer Gannon.

name: October 2024 Storm
notes: Aurora visible over NYC, Chicago, and as far south as Florida.

name: New Year's Storm
notes: CMEs from Dec 29 hit Earth on Dec 31/Jan 1, G4 lasting 9+ hours. Aurora seen as far south as Mexico.

name: June 2025 Storm
notes: G4 watch issued following powerful CME on May 30.

name: November 2025 Storm
notes: G3 observed, G4 expected. Triggered by one of the strongest flares of Solar Cycle 25 on Nov 11.

name: Halloween Storms
notes: Series of extreme storms Oct 29–Nov 4. X17 and X28 flares. Power outages in Sweden, satellite damage.

name: Quebec Storm
notes: Caused 9-hour blackout across Quebec. Aurora seen as far south as Texas and Florida.

name: May 2005 Storm
notes: G5 storm following X-class flares. Final G5 before May 2024.

name: September 2005 Storm
notes: Third G5 event of 2005, the last series before Solar Cycle 25.

name: St. Patrick's Day Storm
notes: Largest storm of Solar Cycle 24. Triggered without a major X-class flare — a coronal hole stream event.

name: Starlink Storm
notes: G3 storm caused SpaceX to lose up to 40 Starlink satellites shortly after launch.

name: September 2017 Storm
notes: G3 storm coinciding with intense X-class flare period. Radio blackouts across the US.

name: November 2003 Storm
notes: Tail end of Halloween storm series. Intense aurora at very low latitudes.
